role_title: Revenue Operations Business Partner - Technical Success

level_signals: Title includes 'Manager' in role description body
Expected to lead cross-functional projects and present to leadership/executives
Requires executive presence and autonomous operation
Expansive ownership and ruthless prioritization signals mid-senior level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in revenue operations or strategy at a high-growth, technology company
Strong analytical skills with a focus on attention to detail
Exceptional project management skills, with experience leading complex, cross-functional initiatives
Strong communication skills and executive presence
An understanding of the AI landscape, our applications, and the problems they solve for our customers.
The ability to thrive in ambiguity and work autonomously
The ability to operate with high horsepower, be adept at frequent context switching and working on multiple projects at once with expansive ownership, and ruthless prioritization

scope.surface: Revenue Operations supporting the Technical Success team within OpenAI's GTM organization
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise AI products and APIs
primary_metrics_inferred: Technical Success team KPIs and dashboards
core_responsibilities: Design and implement processes to support the OpenAI Technical Success team, ensuring seamless management of customer interactions throughout the lifecycle, from onboarding to ongoing technical support and services delivery.
Work closely with technical teams to ensure that operational processes are scalable and efficiently integrated into customer success workflows.
Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) specific to technical success, developing and maintaining dashboards to provide actionable insights to stakeholders and leadership.
Manage capacity and coverage strategy for the Technical Success team, working with leadership to ensure alignment with business objectives and offering data-driven recommendations for improvements.
Partner with the data team to ensure the integrity and accuracy of data, enabling effective reporting and decision-making for revenue-generating activities.
Lead strategic analyses to identify trends, gaps, and opportunities for process optimization; lead cross-functional projects to drive those optimizations.

green_flags: Clear articulation of responsibilities and team context
OpenAI is a high-profile AI company with significant market presence
Role has broad ownership and strategic impact (capacity strategy, KPIs, cross-functional leadership)
Explicit equal opportunity employer statement and accommodation policy
red_flags: Location/remote policy not stated anywhere in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
All requirements framed as 'you might thrive if you have' — ambiguous whether items are truly hard requirements or preferences
Title in identity fields is 'Revenue Operations Business Partner' but JD body repeatedly uses 'Revenue Operations Manager' — potential title inconsistency
No years of experience minimum stated, making seniority expectations opaque

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from leadership expectations, executive presence, autonomous operation, and cross-functional scope)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and GTM team context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as metrics in JD)
ambiguities: All must-have requirements are introduced with 'You might thrive in this role if you have:' — this phrasing suggests soft preferences, but the items are unconditional and substantive, consistent with hard requirements. Treated as hard per schema rules; flagged here.

Role title in identity fields is 'Revenue Operations Business Partner - Technical Success' but the JD body uses 'Revenue Operations Manager' throughout — possible title mismatch between posting metadata and JD content.
Location is entirely unstated — no city, no remote/hybrid/onsite policy mentioned. Location type set to 'unclear'.
The San Francisco and Los Angeles county legal disclosures imply the role may be California-based or have California employees, but this is not a direct statement of work location.
